channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: RFK Jr. says he plans to also meet with Dems in bid to get confirmed as Trump HHS head
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr., a Democrat turned independent, said he plans to meet with Democratic senators in his effort to be secure votes for confirmation as HHS secretary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Archivist says Equal Rights Amendment can't be certified as Democrats push Biden to recognize it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a rare joint statement, the archivist and deputy archivist of the United States said Tuesday that the 1970s-era Equal Rights Amendment cannot be certified without further action by Congress or the courts, as Democrats press President Joe Biden to act unilaterally on its ratifica
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Energy chief Granholm warns against 'unfettered exports' of liquefied natural gas
item.description: Energy Secretary Jennifer Granholm says the incoming Trump administration should proceed cautiously as it considers proposals for new natural gas export terminals.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's transition team eyes expansion of ankle monitors for illegal immigrants not in custody
item.description: The incoming Trump administration is looking at potentially expanding the number of illegal immigrants on GPS monitoring, while also extending the amount of time they are monitored.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AOC loses bid to be top Democrat on powerful House Oversight Committee
item.description: Progressive Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., was defeated in her bid to be the top Democrat on the House Oversight Committee, losing to Rep. Gerry Connolly of Virginia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump seeks to smooth over concerns over RFK Jr
item.description: “I think he’s going to be much less radical than you would think,” Trump said during a wide-ranging news conference at Mar-a-Lago.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Jill Biden leaves job at Virginia community college ahead of White House departure
item.description: Poised to soon leave the White House, first lady Jill Biden announced that she had taught her final class at Northern Virginia Community College.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Connolly defeats Ocasio-Cortez to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly (D-Va.) on Tuesday defeated Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) to lead Democrats on the Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress, putting the feisty liberal veteran in one of the most powerful spots to confront President-elect Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rules Trump's conviction withstands Supreme Court immunity decision
item.description: A New York judge upheld a jury’s verdict that convicted President-elect Trump of a felony, ruling the outcome of the hush money case can withstand the Supreme Court’s new test for presidential immunity.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Senate Democrats push plan to abolish Electoral College
item.description: Three Democratic senators unveiled a constitutional amendment to abolish the Electoral College system Monday, just more than a month after President-elect Trump stunned the Democrats by sweeping all seven battleground states, knocking off three Senate Democratic incumbents in the process. Sens.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says it was 'stupid' for Biden to let Ukraine use US weapons to strike deeper into Russia
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Monday suggested that he may reverse President Joe Biden's recent decision to allow Ukrainian forces to use American long-range weapons to strike deeper into Russian territory. Trump called the decision made by Biden last month “stupid.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams' former chief advisor expects to be indicted: lawyer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ingrid Lewis-Martin, a former top aide to New York Mayor Eric Adams, expects to be indicted on criminal charges related to alleged improper gifts, her lawyer said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Speaker Johnson faces 'complicated' decision on crucial Rules Committee chair
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is barreling into a tricky — yet critical — decision over whom to appoint as the next chair of the House Rules Committee, a post that will set the tone for legislating and play a key role in keeping Republicans united in next year’s ultrathin majority.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Trump claims a 'massive' mandate, but presidents often overread their victories
item.description: There's a long history of presidents claiming that their win means the will of the people is behind their agendas. But elections are rarely full-throated endorsements of a candidate's every policy.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Trial over Minnesota House race that could swing control of chamber focuses on lost ballots
item.description: Republicans challenged the election result arguing that a 14-vote margin between the DFL representative who won and his GOP opponent could be made up in a pool of 20 ballots that were accidentally tossed.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: More than 120 House Democrats send letter to Biden urging him to ratify Equal Rights Amendment
item.description: “We must continue our efforts to fully affirm and recognize the equality of rights for all people, regardless of sex, as part of our Constitution, a vital effort that has never been more urgent,” Congress members penned.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Biden urges Congress to 'do something' for gun reform on Sandy Hook anniversary
item.description: Biden in a press release touted his administration's efforts to combat gun violence but argued that there was more to be done — especially as his presidency enters the final stretch.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: Pelosi has hip replacement surgery at a US military hospital in Germany after a fall
item.description: Pelosi, 84, “is well on the mend,” said Ian Krager, a spokesman for the California Democrat, in a statement.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey drones: State senator wants state of emergency
item.description: One politician in New Jersey is calling for a "limited" state of emergency after what he said is a lack of government transparency over the recent unexplained wave of drone sightings.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers tell Apple, Google to prepare for TikTok ban
item.description: TikTok now plans to appeal to the Supreme Court and has asked the D.C. Circuit to put the law on hold while it appeals the case.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Trump hosts Apple CEO at Mar-a-Lago as big tech leaders continue outreach to president-elect
item.description: Trump has said he has spoken with Cook about the company's long-running tax battles with the European Union.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Adams seeks to deport migrants who commit crimes
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams told President-elect Donald Trump’s border czar he would work with him to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in New York City. Adams met with Tom Homan at Gracie Mansion to discuss Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: NYC Council speaker concerned about deportation plans
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams has concerns about Mayor Eric Adams' goal to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in the city. Mayor Adams met with incoming border czar Tom Homan on Thursday to discuss President-elect Donald Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz says he’ll propose anti-fraud measures, reflects on VP bid
item.description: In his first interview with MPR News since his vice presidential run, Tim Walz said he still has “privilege” of serving as Minnesota governor, urged lawmakers to work together in divided government.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Ontario to restrict electricity exports to U.S. and bar American-made alcohol if Trump tariffs applied
item.description: Ontario may considering barring American-made alcohol in addition to restricting electricity exports to Minnesota, Michigan and New York state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Jacob Frey vetoes 2025 budget approved by Minneapolis City Council
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey announced Wednesday afternoon that he is vetoing the 2025 budget approved by the city council late Tuesday night. The budget had passed with enough votes to override a veto.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News
